heading: From Start To Finish
subheading: Comprehensive surveying services for every phase of your project.

categories.name: Surveying & Mapping
categories.pageSubheading: Precision surveying and mapping solutions for infrastructure, land development, and construction.
categories.pageImageAlt: Surveying and mapping team working in the field

services.name: Geodetic & Control Surveying
bullets: Set primary and secondary horizontal and vertical control using GPS/GNSS and conventional methods to ensure project-wide accuracy.
Provide geodetic positioning tied to state plane coordinates and vertical datums for consistent, reliable survey data.
Support design, construction, and monitoring with dependable control that meets FDOT and industry standards.

services.name: Right-of-Way Surveys and Mapping
bullets: Research deeds, plats, and records to accurately define existing right-of-way and property boundaries.
Prepare detailed right-of-way maps showing parcels, easements, and ownership information to support acquisition and design.
Assist with right-of-way acquisition processes while ensuring compliance with FDOT and local requirements.

services.name: Topographic / As-built / 3D (DTM) Surveys
bullets: Capture existing ground features, elevations, and site conditions to create an accurate representation of the project area.
Generate precise 3D surface models from survey data to support design, grading, and analysis.
Provide reliable topo and DTM data aligned with FDOT and local standards for planning, drainage, and construction.

services.name: Bridge Survey
bullets: Perform thorough bridge surveys to document geometry, structural elements, and existing conditions.
Capture elevations, clearances, and critical measurements to support design, inspection, and rehabilitation efforts.
Deliver survey data aligned with FDOT and industry standards to ensure safe, compliant, and efficient project development.

services.name: Drainage
bullets: Conduct detailed drainage surveys to evaluate existing systems, identify deficiencies, and document site conditions.
Gather accurate elevation data, pipe conditions, and flow patterns to support design and improve system performance.
Provide insights aligned with FDOT and local standards to support drainage design, maintenance planning, and regulatory compliance.

categories.name: Subsurface Utility Engineering (SUE)
categories.pageSubheading: Reliable subsurface utility engineering services that reduce risk and improve design confidence.
categories.pageCardSubheading: All quality levels are defined in accordance with ASCE 38-22, Standard Guideline for Investigating and Documenting Existing Utilities.
categories.pageImageAlt: Subsurface utility engineering crew locating underground utilities

services.name: Quality Level A (QLA) - Utility Locates
bullets: Confirms Utility Locates/ Possible Conflicts through direct exposure at specific points.
Verifies depth, size, material, and survey accuracies (Horizontal & Vertical) where exact data is required.
Helps reduce design/construction risk in critical conflict areas.

services.name: Quality Level B (QLB) – Utility Designates
bullets: Uses surface geophysics to Utility Designate existing underground utilities.
Maps horizontal utility positions without full exposure using Electromagnetic Detection/ Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR).
Supports design decisions.

services.name: Quality Level C (QLC)  – Correlated Records to Surveyed Features
bullets: Correlates Utility Records to Surveyed Features.
Improves the usefulness of existing owner data through field surveys.
Provides a practical planning-level view of underground conditions.

services.name: Quality Level D (QLD) – Existing Records
bullets: Compiles utility information from Existing Records and verbal sources.
Provides an early understanding of potential underground infrastructure.
Best suited for preliminary planning before higher-confidence investigation.

categories.name: Utility Coordination (UC)
categories.pageSubheading: Utility coordination services that keep stakeholders aligned and projects moving forward.
categories.pageImageAlt: Utility coordination field work and infrastructure review

services.name: Utility Coordination
bullets: We assist FDOT, local agencies, contractors, and engineers to deliver projects on time by identifying utility conflicts early and coordinating solutions efficiently.
We offer utility conflict identification, review Utility Work Schedules (UWS), coordinate with utility providers, assist with permitting and compliance, and construction phase support.
Experienced with FDOT requirements, including UAM, CPAM, FDOT Design Manual, UWHC, and SUE integration, keeping your project compliant and moving forward.

services.name: Management of Relocations and Adjustments
bullets: We coordinate utility relocations and adjustments for projects with FDOT and local agencies on behalf of utility owners, ensuring all utility work is planned, scheduled, and completed without delaying construction.

Services include utility relocation planning and scheduling, adjustment coordination during roadway construction, Utility Work Schedule (UWS) management, and coordination with utility owners and contractors.
Experienced with FDOT processes including Utility Work by Highway Contractor (UWHC), right-of-way coordination, and construction phasing, ensuring relocations align with project timelines.
Uncoordinated relocations can delay projects, increase costs, and disrupt construction flow. We ensure utility work is completed at the right time before it becomes a problem.

services.name: Keep Utility Work Aligned with Construction
Environmental Permitting Coordination
bullets: We support the Florida Department of Transportation, local agencies, and contractors with efficient environmental permitting coordination, keeping projects compliant and moving forward.
Services include environmental permit coordination and tracking, agency coordination (state and federal), wetland and surface permitting support, and NEPA documentation coordination.

Experienced with the Florida Department of Environmental Protection, U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, and water management districts across Florida, we help navigate complex requirements and streamline approvals.

Projects often impact wetlands, floodplains, and protected resources. Delays in permitting can significantly impact schedules and costs, so we help ensure permits are secured efficiently and correctly the first time.

categories.name: 3D Laser Scanning (LiDAR)
categories.pageSubheading: Advanced LiDAR and scanning solutions for accurate site documentation, modeling, and analysis.
categories.pageImageAlt: LiDAR scanning equipment capturing site conditions

services.name: Static LiDar (Topo/DTM)
bullets: Captures dense, high accuracy laser scan data from fixed ground positions in a time efficient manner.
Ideal for use on bridges, roadways, interior and exterior structures, and a wide array of as-built surveys.
Produces highly detailed data that can be used for measurements, design, modeling and analysis.

services.name: Mobile LiDAR (Topo/DTM)
bullets: Collects LiDAR data from vehicle mounted systems along roadway, railway or maritime corridors.
Efficiently captures roadways, utilities and surrounding infrastructure with high accuracy and detail.
Greatly reduces field time and overall project costs.

services.name: Point Cloud Modeling (TopoDOT)
bullets: Transforms point clouds into clear and usable drawings and 3D models.
Extracts all visible components of the job site to provide a complete and detailed view.
Supports design teams with clean deliverables from complex scan data.

services.name: Aerial-Drones
bullets: Uses drones to capture aerial imagery and site-wide elevation data.
Supports terrain modeling, progress tracking, and visual documentation.
Provides efficient coverage for large or hard-to-access areas.

categories.name: Construction
categories.pageSubheading: Construction surveying services that support layout, verification, and project control in the field.
categories.pageImageAlt: Construction surveying team on an active project site

services.name: Construction Layout & Staking
bullets: Places field control points for grading, utilities, and structural work.
Translates design plans into accurate on-site construction layout.
Helps crews build to the intended line and grade.

services.name: As-Built Surveys
bullets: Documents completed construction in its installed location.
Compares built conditions against design intent and project records.
Supports closeout, verification, and future maintenance needs.

services.name: Settlement Monitoring
bullets: Monitors movement over time in soil, structures, or infrastructure.
Identifies settlement trends early so teams can respond quickly.
Supports risk management during and after construction.

services.name: Earthwork Calculations
bullets: Calculates cut, fill, and material quantities from survey data.
Tracks earthwork progress throughout the life of the project.
Helps improve budgeting, haul planning, and production control.

categories.name: Residential/Commercial
categories.pageSubheading: Survey solutions for residential and commercial properties, permitting, and property due diligence.
categories.pageImageAlt: Residential and commercial surveying at a developed property

services.name: Boundary Survey and Elevation (FEMA) Certificate
bullets: Establish boundary lines and key site improvements with accuracy and precision.
Provides elevation information needed for FEMA-related documentation.
Helps owners, lenders, and insurers understand site conditions.

services.name: Platting Survey
bullets: Creates or retraces lot layouts for subdivision and land development.
Defines parcel dimensions, corners, and related right-of-way to meet zoning requirements.
Supports entitlement, recordation, and future property transfer.
Provides compliance with municipal and county regulations.

services.name: ALTA/NSPS Land Title Survey
bullets: Meets ALTA/NSPS standards for commercial property transactions.
Documents boundary evidence, improvements, easements, and access.
Supports title review, financing, and due diligence.

services.name: FDEP / Mean High Water Survey
bullets: Determines shoreline or jurisdictional water-related survey limits.
Supports permitting and environmental coordination requirements.
Provides defensible data for coastal and waterfront projects.
We handle FDEP approvals and filings of MHW Surveys to ensure compliance of the project.
